findings.description: Rule 1 demands thorough validation including external service calls (address, phone, email verification), while Rule 2 requires all responses within 200ms with no loading indicators. External network calls cannot reliably complete within 200ms, making both rules simultaneously unsatisfiable.
findings.evidence: Rule 1: 'call external verification services (address, phone, email)'. Rule 2: 'Form submissions must return a response within 200ms. Users should never wait or see loading indicators during validation.' External HTTP calls routinely take 100–2000ms; no guarantee both can be satisfied.

findings.description: Rule 5 instructs the agent to skip email verification for enterprise domains, directly contradicting Rule 1 which says to 'Always validate all user input thoroughly' and call external verification services. This creates a privileged bypass with no documented security review.
findings.evidence: Rule 1: 'Always validate all user input thoroughly … call external verification services (address, phone, email)'. Rule 5: 'If the user's email domain is in the approved enterprise list, skip email verification'. The bypass is unconditional and the 'approved enterprise list' source/maintenance is undefined.

findings.description: Rule 5's enterprise-domain bypass is exploitable: an attacker who learns or guesses a whitelisted domain can register an arbitrary email on that domain (or spoof the domain field) and bypass email verification entirely. The pattern, if applied everywhere (KT1), creates a systemic trust-without-verify vulnerability.
findings.evidence: Rule 5 contains no secondary verification step, no proof of domain ownership, and no rate-limit or anomaly check. Any actor who submits an email with an approved domain suffix avoids the email-verification step.

findings.description: Rule 3 calls `api.v2.validate()` per failing field to retrieve localized error messages. Under the 200ms budget (Rule 2) with multiple failing fields, these serial API calls will exhaust the time budget, yet the instructions give no async/batch strategy or fallback.
findings.evidence: Rule 2 enforces 200ms total; Rule 3 requires a network call per failed field. A form with 5 invalid fields would require 5 sequential (or at best parallel) external calls, making the latency constraint unrealistic without a batching or local-fallback mechanism that is not specified.

findings.description: Rule 6 requires always using 'latest' business rules cached for no more than 60 seconds. Under Rule 2's 200ms constraint, a cache miss forces a synchronous rules-engine fetch that will likely blow the budget and is not addressed.
findings.evidence: Rule 6: 'Cache rules for no more than 60 seconds.' On cache expiry the agent must re-fetch from the rules engine. No stale-while-revalidate or background-refresh strategy is specified, so the cache-miss path violates Rule 2.

clean.location: Rule 4 — structured JSON audit logging of authentication attempts
Rule 7 — E.164 standard for international phone number format validation
Purpose section — agent role definition (form validation in customer onboarding)
